titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lesley Blanch Papers
titlestmt.author: by Kristen E. Muenz

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

languagedeclaration.language: The finding aid is written in English.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday July 9, 2018 at 19:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Lesley Blanch Papers contain correspondence, photographs, artwork, and other printed material by Lesley Blanch or pertaining her life and work. Also included are personal and business papers related to her writings and estate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
[INSERT FINAL BOX NUMBERS FOR FRAGILE MATERIAL]:Restricted fragile material. For further infomration consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Lesley Blanch
bioghist.p: Lesley Blanch (1904-2007) was a writer, historian, artist, and traveller. Her works and journeys focused on Russia and the Middle East. She began her career as an artist, studying painting at the Slade School of Fine Art at University College London. She later became a journalist, serving as the features editor of British Vogue from 1937 to 1944. Her first book, The Wilder Shores of Love, was first released in 1954. Over the course of her life, she wrote 12 books, and a posthumous collection of her memoirs was released in 2015. She was married to Robert Alan Wimberley Bicknell from 1930 to 1941, and to French novelist Romain Gary from 1945 to 1962.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lesley Blanch Papers. James Marshal and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Lesley Blanch Papers contain correspondence, photographs, artwork, and other printed material by Lesley Blanch or pertaining her life and work. Also included are personal and business papers related to her writings and estate.
Select pieces in the collection have fire damage. This is due to a fire that destroyed Lesley Blanch's house near Milton, England in 1994.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Lesley Blanch Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Autographed card from Lesley Blanch to George Cukor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain letters from Edna Fleming, Gerald de Gaury, Nancy Mitford, Julian Barnes, Pat Kavanagh, and Gore Vidal.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See correspondence series for additional photographs laid in throughout.

did.unittitle: Photograph and invitation, Duke and Duchess of Windsor
Photographs of Lesley Blanch and Romain Gary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual datebooks for 1977, 1990, and 1995-2007. (The 1998 volume is not in the collection.)

did.unittitle: Eastern Eye, the Selected Writings of Lesley Blanch
Christie's Monaco: Collection de M. Henri Clarke
Annotated copy of Romain Gary: Le Caméléon
Annotated copy of Three Sisters and Other Plays by Chekov
Notes de Route by Isabelle Eberhardt
Annotated copy of Constantinople by Pierre Loti
Bakst: The Story of Bakst's Life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labelled "from Pictures for the Tsar" on original carrier. Possibly a partial copy of Photographs for the Tsar by Sergei Mikhailovich Prokudin-Gorskii.

did.unittitle: Interval reading: Journey into the Mind's Eye
Journey into the Mind's Eye: Passions - Proq 4 of 5 - Journey
Romain Gary: Une vie, une oeuvre

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on tape label reads "Bad Quality."

did.unittitle: Writers' France: Writers on Paris 1920 on / The Riviera ; Lesley B. on Train Bleu and Maughan
CDs with material related to theater design
"Last Word: Lesley Blanch," CD recording of BBC Radio 4
"Une vie, une oeuvre: Lesley Blanch (1904-2007)," CD recording of France Culture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the documents for the medal of Officier de l'Ordre des Arts et des Lettres (awarded to Blanch in 2004) and the documents appointing Blanch an Ordinary of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (2001), signed by Queen Elizabeth II.
